runConditions.note: Local run — see the disclaimers before comparing numbers across environments.

provenance.trust: docs-sourced from designsystem.gov.ae — provisional, needs revalidation against the live page before each release; TDRA updates the checklist with design-system releases

machineCheckedItems.question: Has the design team created hidden design elements such as "Skip to content"

findings.message: No "skip to content" link detected among in-page anchors (heuristic — checked link text/class/target in English and Arabic).
findings.fix: Add a (visually hidden, focus-visible) Skip to content as the first focusable element.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you created user-friendly error pages for 404, 403, 500

findings.message: The 404 response for an unknown URL looks like a bare server default (1245 bytes) — not the user-friendly designed error page the checklist asks for (404/403/500).
findings.fix: Serve the designed 404 page (site navigation, bilingual message, link home); do the same for 403/500.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you built your frontend using TailwindCSS classes?
Is your frontend code structurally hierarchical using the accurate placement of <h1> to <h6> tags?

findings.message: Heading levels should only increase by one
findings.fix:
Fix any of the following:
  Heading order invalid

findings.message: Page should contain a level-one heading
findings.fix:
Fix all of the following:
  Page must have a level-one heading

No AEGOV Design Language System components detected on this page (zero aegov-* classes) — the UI appears hand-rolled or built on a third-party framework. The standard requires official DLS components and tokens over hand-rolled equivalents. Review whether this page is in scope for the DLS.
findings.fix: Adopt @aegov/design-system components and tokens for the page's UI.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you added the "aria-hidden="true" attribute to icons that must not be read by screen readers?

findings.message: 10 icon element(s) are neither aria-hidden="true" nor given an accessible name — screen readers may announce them meaninglessly.
findings.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to decorative icons; give meaningful icons an accessible name.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured all icons have a supporting text - either visible or wrapped in the "sr-only" class?

findings.message: 10 icon-only link(s)/button(s) carry no supporting text — neither visible, sr-only, nor aria-label.
findings.fix: Add visible text, a .sr-only span, or an aria-label to every icon-only control.

machineCheckedItems.question: Are you adding accurate aria attributes to form elements and labels?
Have you tested your frontend code to be AA Level compliant with WCAG 2.1

findings.message: Elements must meet minimum color contrast ratio thresholds
findings.fix:
Fix any of the following:
  Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.88 (foreground color: #9e978f, background color: #ffffff, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: bold). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

findings.message: Images must have alternative text
findings.fix:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have an alt attribute
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

findings.message: Links must have discernible text
findings.fix:
Fix all of the following:
  Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text

Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has an empty title attribute

findings.message: Scrollable region must have keyboard access
findings.fix:
Fix any of the following:
  Element should have focusable content
  Element should be focusable

machineCheckedItems.question: Is your hero block using <picture> with srcset to add a different image for mobile and desktop?
Have you used the login block as described with UAE Pass as the primary login method?
Is the Doctype HTML5 on the top of all pages?
Is the charset declared as UTF-8?
Is the viewport declared accurately?
Has a favicon been included with variations for bookmarking sizes?
Are you using the Apple-meta tags to control the colour of your website's pre header?

findings.message: No theme-color / Apple status-bar meta tag — the browser pre-header colour is not controlled.
findings.fix: Add <meta name="theme-color" content="…"> (and the Apple status-bar variant).

machineCheckedItems.question: Are you using "rel=canonical" to avoid duplicate content page indexing?

findings.message: No rel=canonical link — review whether duplicate-content indexing is possible on this page.
findings.fix: Add <link rel="canonical" href="…"> when the same content is reachable under several URLs.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured adding the "direction" attribute to your body tag according to your language?

findings.message: Arabic text renders in an LTR context (<h3.heading> "خدمات الشركات") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message:
Arabic text renders in an LTR context (

"ستكشف مجموعة خدمات الشركة المقدمة من قبل منظمتنا.") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message: Arabic text renders in an LTR context (<a.btn-learn-head.mt-3> "اعرف المزيد") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message: Arabic text renders in an LTR context (<h3.heading> "خدمات النقليات") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message: Arabic text renders in an LTR context (<a.btn-learn-head.mt-3> "اعرف المزيد") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message: Arabic text renders in an LTR context (<h3.heading> "دليل الخدمات") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message:
Arabic text renders in an LTR context (

"نتقل إلى دليل خدماتنا واختر الخدمة التي تناسب احتياجاتك بشكل") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message: Arabic text renders in an LTR context (<a.btn-learn-head.mt-3> "اعرف المزيد") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.

findings.message: Arabic text renders in an LTR context (<h3.heading> "خدمات للأفراد") with no dir="rtl" in scope and no explicit lang="ar" inline tagging — RTL is first-class in the standard; review the direction handling.
findings.fix: Establish dir="rtl" on the Arabic content (or tag a short inline island with lang="ar").

findings.message: No direction attribute on the document although Arabic content/language is present — RTL is first-class in the standard.
findings.fix: Set dir="rtl" on the Arabic version and dir="ltr" on the English version.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured added the "lang" attribute to your HTML tag based on your language?

findings.message: The document declares lang="en" but the visible text is predominantly Arabic (82% Arabic script) — the declared language must match the content.
findings.fix: Serve the Arabic variant with <html lang="ar" dir="rtl"> (and the English variant with lang="en").

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured all pages list the alternate language tag?

findings.message: No alternate-language link tags (<link rel="alternate" hreflang=…>) — pages should list their other-language version.
findings.fix: Add hreflang alternate links on every page of both language versions.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured you have the facility to generate open meta tags for social media?

findings.message: No Open Graph meta tags (og:*) on the page — social-media shares will not carry controlled title/image.
findings.fix: Emit og:title, og:description and og:image per page (the CMS should generate them).

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you structured your HTML using HTML5 semantic tags (such as <header>, <footer>, <section>, <main>)
Have you created the Error pages - 404, 403, 500

findings.message: The 404 response for an unknown URL looks like a bare server default (1245 bytes) — not the user-friendly designed error page the checklist asks for (404/403/500).
findings.fix: Serve the designed 404 page (site navigation, bilingual message, link home); do the same for 403/500.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured you are following the design system guideline on use of hyperlinks to add "rel=noopener" where required?

findings.message: 17 link(s) with target="_blank" lack rel="noopener" — modern browsers imply it, but the design-system guideline asks for it explicitly.
findings.fix: Add rel="noopener" (or "noopener noreferrer") to links opening new tabs.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured the use of Google Fonts and not loading fonts from your server?
Have you ensured that CSS is NON-Blocking?
Are you minifying your rendered CSS and JavaScript for a production environment?

findings.message: Unminified CSS shipped to production (Lighthouse unminified-css; mobile: Est savings of 27 KiB; desktop: Est savings of 27 KiB).
findings.fix: Minify rendered CSS in the production build pipeline.

findings.message: Unminified JavaScript shipped to production (Lighthouse unminified-javascript; mobile: Est savings of 54 KiB; desktop: Est savings of 54 KiB).
findings.fix: Minify rendered JavaScript in the production build pipeline.

machineCheckedItems.question: Are you implementing a cache system for faster page load?
Are all images dynamically adding an alt tag that is meaningful to the image?

findings.message: Images must have alternative text
findings.fix:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have an alt attribute
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

alt="Image Placeholder" on 16 image(s) (e.g. src="https://apps.fnrc.gov.ae/website_AdminPanel/Content/Dynamic-Content/Banner-Image") looks like a CMS placeholder or filename, not a description — it passes automated alt checks while conveying nothing (WCAG 1.1.1). Review and replace with real alternative text.
findings.fix: Write alternative text that describes the image's content or function; use alt="" only for purely decorative images.

findings.message: alt="Alternate Text" on 6 image(s) (e.g. src="https://apps.fnrc.gov.ae/website_AdminPanel/Content/Dynamic-Content/Banner-Image") looks like a CMS placeholder or filename, not a description — it passes automated alt checks while conveying nothing (WCAG 1.1.1). Review and replace with real alternative text.
findings.fix: Write alternative text that describes the image's content or function; use alt="" only for purely decorative images.

machineCheckedItems.question: Are you using Picture/Srcset for images as described in the design system?

findings.message: None of the 120 images use srcset or <picture> — no responsive image delivery as the design system describes.
findings.fix: Serve size-appropriate variants via srcset/sizes or <picture>.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you enabled lazy loading for images?

findings.message: None of the 120 images use loading="lazy" — below-the-fold images all load eagerly.
findings.fix: Add loading="lazy" to images below the fold (keep the LCP/hero image eager).

machineCheckedItems.question: Are you using WebP as your image format primarily?

findings.message: 78 images resolve to JPEG/PNG/GIF by URL and none to WebP/AVIF (extension-based check — a CDN content-negotiating formats would not show here; verify the actual response types).
findings.fix: Serve WebP (with fallbacks via <picture>) as the primary image format.

machineCheckedItems.question: Are you videos being loaded based on user's device and internet speed? This would happen by default if you are hosting public videos on Youtube or Vimeo
Eliminating all images on the page, is your page weight below 500KB while loading?

findings.message: Page weight excluding images exceeds the TDRA budget of 500 KB (checklist 3.53): mobile 1.51 MB, desktop 2.23 MB. Transfer size of this local load, images subtracted.
findings.fix: Trim non-image payload: split/defer JavaScript, remove unused CSS, subset fonts, enable text compression.

machineCheckedItems.question: With all images on your page, is your page weight below 4MB while loading?

findings.message: Total page weight exceeds the TDRA budget of 4 MB (checklist 3.54, read as 4.00 MB): mobile 8.51 MB, desktop 9.24 MB. Transfer size of this local load.
findings.fix: Reduce transferred bytes: compress/resize images, remove unused scripts and styles, enable text compression.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you ensured JavaScript is placed at the end of your website?

findings.message: 3 script(s) load synchronously in <head> (no defer/async/module): https://cdn.datatables.net/1.11.5/js/jquery.dataTables.min.js, https://www.google.com/recaptcha/enterprise.js?render=6LeMlEAoAAAAAPWFzJoe5ZKrykUPurUORg17zOMH, https://translate.googleapis.com/_/translate_http/_/js/k=translate_http.tr.ar.bjD-3sDy_BI.O/am=BIAABw/d=1/exm=el_conf/ed=1/rs=AN8SPfpGhsP7HnZaM1nB5I9gjOQLrTql6Q/m=el_main — the checklist asks for JavaScript at the end of the page.
findings.fix: Move scripts before </body> or mark them defer/async.

machineCheckedItems.question: Have you taken into account third party scripts and software that will affect your load time in a production environment?
Have you added a cookie banner?
Does your content management system generate a Sitemap.xml file?

findings.message: No sitemap found: https://fnrc.gov.ae/sitemap.xml answers HTTP 404 and robots.txt declares no Sitemap
findings.fix: Generate sitemap.xml from the CMS and declare it in robots.txt.

tdraChecklist.note: Mizan machine-checks 39 of 125 checklist items (fully or partially). "No automated findings" covers the machine-checkable subset only — it is NOT a pass; the remaining items are process/design questions a human answers. Items marked "not checked" had no evidence engine in this run (they need --lighthouse and/or an http(s) target for the origin probes).

findings.message: No Open Graph meta tags (og:*) on the page — social-media shares will not carry controlled title/image.
findings.fix: Emit og:title, og:description and og:image per page (the CMS should generate them).

findings.message: 17 link(s) with target="_blank" lack rel="noopener" — modern browsers imply it, but the design-system guideline asks for it explicitly.
findings.fix: Add rel="noopener" (or "noopener noreferrer") to links opening new tabs.

findings.message: 3 script(s) load synchronously in <head> (no defer/async/module): https://cdn.datatables.net/1.11.5/js/jquery.dataTables.min.js, https://www.google.com/recaptcha/enterprise.js?render=6LeMlEAoAAAAAPWFzJoe5ZKrykUPurUORg17zOMH, https://translate.googleapis.com/_/translate_http/_/js/k=translate_http.tr.ar.bjD-3sDy_BI.O/am=BIAABw/d=1/exm=el_conf/ed=1/rs=AN8SPfpGhsP7HnZaM1nB5I9gjOQLrTql6Q/m=el_main — the checklist asks for JavaScript at the end of the page.
findings.fix: Move scripts before </body> or mark them defer/async.

findings.message: No "skip to content" link detected among in-page anchors (heuristic — checked link text/class/target in English and Arabic).
findings.fix: Add a (visually hidden, focus-visible) Skip to content as the first focusable element.

findings.message: 10 icon element(s) are neither aria-hidden="true" nor given an accessible name — screen readers may announce them meaninglessly.
findings.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to decorative icons; give meaningful icons an accessible name.

findings.message: 10 icon-only link(s)/button(s) carry no supporting text — neither visible, sr-only, nor aria-label.
findings.fix: Add visible text, a .sr-only span, or an aria-label to every icon-only control.

findings.message: None of the 120 images use srcset or <picture> — no responsive image delivery as the design system describes.
findings.fix: Serve size-appropriate variants via srcset/sizes or <picture>.

findings.message: None of the 120 images use loading="lazy" — below-the-fold images all load eagerly.
findings.fix: Add loading="lazy" to images below the fold (keep the LCP/hero image eager).

findings.message: 78 images resolve to JPEG/PNG/GIF by URL and none to WebP/AVIF (extension-based check — a CDN content-negotiating formats would not show here; verify the actual response types).
findings.fix: Serve WebP (with fallbacks via <picture>) as the primary image format.

findings.message: No sitemap found: https://fnrc.gov.ae/sitemap.xml answers HTTP 404 and robots.txt declares no Sitemap
findings.fix: Generate sitemap.xml from the CMS and declare it in robots.txt.

findings.message: The 404 response for an unknown URL looks like a bare server default (1245 bytes) — not the user-friendly designed error page the checklist asks for (404/403/500).
findings.fix: Serve the designed 404 page (site navigation, bilingual message, link home); do the same for 403/500.

findings.message: Total page weight exceeds the TDRA budget of 4 MB (checklist 3.54, read as 4.00 MB): mobile 8.51 MB, desktop 9.24 MB. Transfer size of this local load.
findings.fix: Reduce transferred bytes: compress/resize images, remove unused scripts and styles, enable text compression.

findings.message: Page weight excluding images exceeds the TDRA budget of 500 KB (checklist 3.53): mobile 1.51 MB, desktop 2.23 MB. Transfer size of this local load, images subtracted.
findings.fix: Trim non-image payload: split/defer JavaScript, remove unused CSS, subset fonts, enable text compression.

findings.message: Unminified CSS shipped to production (Lighthouse unminified-css; mobile: Est savings of 27 KiB; desktop: Est savings of 27 KiB).
findings.fix: Minify rendered CSS in the production build pipeline.

findings.message: Unminified JavaScript shipped to production (Lighthouse unminified-javascript; mobile: Est savings of 54 KiB; desktop: Est savings of 54 KiB).
findings.fix: Minify rendered JavaScript in the production build pipeline.

disclaimers: Community tool. Not affiliated with or endorsed by TDRA.
Automated checks cover only a machine-checkable subset of the standard — a clean automated run is NOT compliance and NOT WCAG 2.1/2.2 AA conformance.
Arabic/RTL parity findings are flags for native-speaker review; Mizan never asserts parity as settled.
Lighthouse numbers were produced under the run conditions recorded in this report (local machine, simulated throttling) — they are comparable across local runs, not to TDRA's environment.
This is a private report for the site's own team.
